title: Tenantly
summary: Multi-tenant website builder I'm building on Laravel for a US company.
problem: Small businesses want their own sites without touching code, and the company needed to run thousands of them from one codebase.
approach: Building the multi-tenant Laravel backend — tenant isolation, billing, a Vue editor — with a publishing pipeline that pushes sites to the edge on Cloudflare. I picked Laravel because the team already knew it; a faster stack nobody could maintain wasn't worth it.
result: Runs ~1,200 tenant sites today, and a new site goes live in under 30 seconds.

title: Steady — Habit Tracker
summary: Solo-built React Native habit and sobriety tracker, live on the iOS App Store.
problem: I wanted a habit and sobriety tracker that didn't feel like a to-do list. So I built one.
approach: Designed and built the whole app solo: habit creation, streak logic, milestone badges, and auto-renewing subscriptions through Apple In-App Purchase.
result: On the App Store with ~4,000 downloads and a paying subscriber base, all built and shipped by me.

title: Precinct
summary: US voter-information platform for finding legislators, candidates, and results by precinct.
problem: Voters can't easily find precinct-level information on legislators, ballot candidates, and election results in one place.
approach: Maintained and extended the platform through its 2.x cycle — added SMS and email alerts for seat changes and election timers, and rebuilt the subscription tiers.
result: Shipped a three-tier model (Free / State $9/mo / National $99/mo), migrated the infrastructure, and cleared the stability bugs across the release cycle.

title: Threadline CRM
summary: Full-stack CRM with unified email and social messaging plus a companion mobile app.
problem: Sales teams need email and social messages in one CRM, on web and on mobile.
approach: Built it on Next.js, Supabase, and GCE; wired up Gmail and Facebook APIs for unified messaging; shipped a React Native companion app; instrumented with Segment and Sentry.
result: Delivered the messaging and mobile features in a five-person cross-functional team.

title: Kaizen Sport — Eligibility Wizard
summary: Youth-athlete eligibility tool with reporting and automated NCAA updates.
problem: Managing youth-athlete eligibility and NCAA status meant heavy manual analysis and updates.
approach: Led a four-dev team to re-architect the app on Next.js and Tailwind, built charting with PDF export, and added a React/Node email and SMS notification system.
result: Cut data-analysis time by 30% and manual NCAA updates by 50%.

title: Wizzer Training
summary: Training-course site rebuilt for speed with a 10-plus-person team.
problem: A content-heavy training site was loading too slowly on web and on mobile.
approach: Built it on React and Node.js with asset optimization, lazy loading, and code-splitting, and automated training reminders through GCP.
result: Cut web boot time by 80% and mobile load time by 30%.

title: Transore Storefront
summary: Go storefront API rewrite for a Shopify-based platform.
problem: The storefront API was slow and had to keep up with Shopify market changes.
approach: Rewrote the storefront API in Go, built market-insight and redirect features, and migrated the platform onto new Shopify markets.

title: Landing Page Service
summary: Conversion-focused landing pages wired to ad campaigns and analytics for multiple clients.
problem: Clients running Google and Meta ads needed fast landing pages plus the hosting and tracking to measure them.
approach: Built and deployed landing pages across client accounts, ran the hosting and server infrastructure, and set up analytics and conversion-tracking pipelines.
result: Shipped 20-plus campaign pages across multiple clients with end-to-end hosting and conversion tracking.
